rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Vlad Cood'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday June 20, 2018 line 1. Plaintiff Vlad Cood'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: LISA J. BAILLY-SOLWAY ET AL VS. HILARY C. PIERCE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday June 20, 2018 line 2. PLAINTIFF LISA BAILLY-SOLWAY, THOMAS PHILLIPS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Judgment or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED.Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Judgment as to Plaintiffs' Complaint is DENIED, see rulings on Motion for Summary Adjudication. Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs' First Cause of Action for nuisance is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to shift the burden. Summary adjudication cannot be granted to a portion of a cause of action. (1) Plaintiffs' nuisance cause of action does not distinguish between access to the electrical breakers and access to the garbage area. (2) Even if access to the electrical breakers constituted a separate cause of action, damages were not shifted. Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs' Second Cause of Action for breach of CC&Rs is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to shift burden. Additionally, there are triable issues of fact as to whether CC&Rs ("the Declaration") entitle Plaintiffs and their tenants 24/7 access to the Utilities Area. (See Decl. Pierce 9-13). Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiffs' Third Cause of Action for declaratory relief is DENIED, for the same reasons as to the First and Second Causes of Action. Plaintiffs' Motion for Summary Judgment or Summary Adjudication as to Defendant's Second Amended Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Defendant's Third Amended Cross-Complaint is the operative cross-complaint. Plaintiffs noticed their Motion for Summary Judgment or Summary Adjudication as to Defendant's Second Amended Cross-Complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 1429 GRANT AVE, LLC, VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday June 20, 2018 line 6. PLAINTIFF 1429 GRANT AVE, LLC, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS This motion has been continued to provide opposing party an opportunity to address Court's updated tentative ruling. Plaintiff's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is GRANTED without leave to amend, subject to Cross-Complainant's showing as to how he can allege a valid lease agreement. The September 30, 2016 Amended Lease Agreement is invalid in light of the June 6, 2016 Bankruptcy Order. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. No objections filed and Cross-Complainant relies on Plaintiff's RJN exhibits in opposition. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS SULLIVAN VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Of Defendants James Nunemacher And Vanguard Properties Inc To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday June 20, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., JAMES NUNEMACHER Motion Of Defendants James Nunemacher And Vanguard Properties Inc Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint DENIED. Motion to Strike Plaintiff's Seventh Cause of Action (FAC, 44-47, Prayer for Relief, 3) is DENIED. The Seventh Cause of Action is within the scope of the Court's previous order sustaining with leave to amend. Motion to Strike punitive damages (FAC, 34; Prayer for Relief, 4) is DENIED. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to support a cause of action for fraud, which is all Civil Code § 3294(a) requires. Motion to Strike prayer for restitution (Prayer for Relief, 2) is DENIED. Plaintiff's Second Cause of Action for Quantum Meruit supports prayer for restitution. Motion to Strike other allegations (FAC, 9-13, 15, 16, 18, 20, 22, 24, 30, 41, 45-47) is DENIED. Defendants ask the Court to make factual determinations by weighing evidence. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS SULLIVAN VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday June 20, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., JAMES NUNEMACHER DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULLED in part. Demurrer to First Cause of Action is OVERRULED, Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts for breach of oral contract. Defendants' asks the Court to make factual determinations by weighing evidence, which is improper on demurrer. For similar reasons, Demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Quantum Meruit and Third Cause of Action for Promissory Estoppel are OVERRULLED. Demurrer to the Fourth and Fifth Causes of Action are OVERRULLED, Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts for fraud and negligent misrepresentation. Demurrer to the Sixth Cause of Action under Business and Professions Code section 17200 is OVERRULED, Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts. Demurrer to the Seventh Cause of Action for Reformation is OVERRULLED, Defendant asks the Court to make factual determinations by weighing evidence. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts for reformation. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN M FRANKLIN VS. UNION AND POLK LP. A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday June 20, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANT ADAMAS DEVELOPMENT, LLC, UNION AND POLK PARTNERS, LP DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend. As to the cause of action for breach of contract Plaintiff shall Plaintiff to attach the contract breached or set verbatim the covenant of the contract containing "contractual duties of disclosure" (Opposition 14:8). As to the causes of action for misrepresentation and concealment Plaintiff shall state with specificity which facts have been misrepresented and by whom; and which facts have been concealed (and by whom, to the best of Plaintiff's ability; e.g. if a particular concealed fact should have been disclosed in a written disclosure, identify the individual who signed the disclosure or attach the disclosure so that such individual may be identified). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON F/K/A THE BANK OF VS. MELE T.A AFUHAAMANGO SINAPOPO
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday June 20, 2018 line 5. DEFENDANT MELE T.A SINAPOPO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer under CCP 1161a and is not uncertain. = (501/REQ)
